lines: The nearer bandit steps between you and the stake, hand on his hilt, eyes flicking to your ears. "Far enough. You walk like you've got business — and we're the only business on this road. State it, knife-ear, before we decide you're cargo too."

choices.label: (Offer 20 gp — buy the two at the stake.)
[Persuasion DC 13] The White Capes are sweeping this wood for casters. You don't want to be standing over roped elves when they ride through.
[Intimidation DC 13] There are two of you, and the wood is very quiet. Walk away — or don't walk at all.

lines: He bites the coin, grunts, and waves his partner back from the stake. "Hah. Paying for what you could've taken — that's elves for you. They're yours, knife-ear." The two of them gather their gear and melt back into the trees.

He looks past you, down the road, and something in his face does the sum. "...Tch. Not worth a Cape's noose, the two of 'em." He spits, jerks his head at his partner. "Keep your strays." They back off the clearing and are gone into the wood.

The smaller one is already stepping back, hands rising. "Easy. Easy — they're not worth dying over." The nearer bandit holds your eye a breath longer, then follows him off into the trees, swearing under his breath.

He smiles, slow and unimpressed. "Words. We've heard a lot of words on this road." His hand tightens on the hilt. "Last chance — pay, or back off this clearing while you've still got the legs for it."

choices.label: (Offer 20 gp — buy the two at the stake.)
Back away — leave them for now.

lines: "Wise," he calls after you, not bothering to follow. "They'll keep. So will we."
"Cargo it is, then." Steel rasps free of leather.
